rationale: Both correctly land on the thread's actual resolution (attribution covered by #3576's stage/replica relabeling; tail_waste/fragmentation withdrawn), but X then confidently 'resolves' the make_stats throttle with an oddly specific, unverifiable regression-test citation that the thread never discussed — a confident claim outside the ground truth. Y instead treats the throttle as a genuinely open, separate concern and escalates it rather than asserting a fix, which is the more defensible move on an unresolved point. Y is also more complete, capturing the LLM_AR/LLM_Generation-vs-diffusion scoping thread that X omits entirely, and grounds its claims with more (still unverifiable but more numerous and consistent) file/line citations.
